definition: PCA dimensionality of G10 FX returns collapses during stress: effective factor count drops from 2.5-3.0 to ~1.2-1.5, with eigenvalue ratio expanding from ~3:1 to ~8:1+. This renders multi-factor diversification illusory in crisis. The indicator is contemporaneous, not predictive, limiting early-warning value but confirming it as a real-time risk measurement.
evidence: Documented across GFC (2008-09), eurozone crisis (2011-12), COVID (2020-03), and rate shock (2022 Q3-Q4)
Both quant researchers agree the indicator is contemporaneous, not predictive
open_questions: Whether PCA compression rate (speed of dimensionality collapse) has predictive content even if level does not
Whether the threshold factor count (e.g., dropping below 2.0) can serve as a real-time risk-off trigger
